subject: [RFC PATCH 03/40] mm: page_alloc: split-path PCP free with local-trylock + remote-llist

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch addresses a lock-inversion risk in the page allocator's PCP free path by introducing a per-CPU remote free list (llist) that allows remote CPUs to enqueue pages for free without taking the zone->lock. The local CPU then absorbs these queued pages back onto its PCP buddy lists at the next opportunity, eliminating the need for a trylock-fallback approach and reducing lock contention. This change also includes various other optimizations, such as memory hot-add lazy init and improved handling of page allocation on dead CPUs.
